refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic functional requirement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) is less than or equal to the customer-defined target for this phase.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times
Implement adaptive control of load equipment to dynamically adjust to site conditions
Design load equipment with capacity and speed parameters aligned to cycle time targets
Integrate real-time monitoring and feedback to adjust load phase execution
Coordinate load phase activities with upstream and downstream processes to reduce delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times

potential_sources: Interdependencies between load phase and travel/dump phases
Variability in site conditions affecting load phase timing
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on the load phase sequence itself, aiming to minimize internal delays and idle times, which supports independence from other phases. However, coordination with other phases and site conditions may introduce coupling that needs management at the implementation level.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time targets are achievable through operational sequence optimization alone
Site conditions and duty cycles remain within expected ranges
Safety plans and operational policies do not impose constraints that significantly extend load phase duration
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including coordination mechanisms with adjacent phases and real-time adaptation to site conditions to further ensure cycle time targets are met under variable conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: This module encompasses the overall system solution including operational sequencing and optimization logic for load phase activities.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational data and sends status updates relevant to load phase optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task assignments to adjust operational sequences.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as load phase optimization is central to the solution logic; minimal diffusion as most related data and control remain internal.
Vehicle module handles physical operations but optimization logic would be fragmented or require extensive external coordination.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not internal sequencing optimization; would cause high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety constraints, not operational sequencing; poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it centralizes the operational sequencing logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical operation constraints are tightly coupled with sequencing logic.
Interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking may increase if optimization requires frequent updates or adjustments.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on optimizing the load phase operational sequence to minimize idle and transition times, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical operation constraints are tightly coupled with sequencing logic.
Interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking may increase if optimization requires frequent updates or adjustments.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module centralizes the operational sequencing logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules, as indicated by high containment and low diffusion scores. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the appropriate IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the customer-defined target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, with well-defined interfaces to Telemetry and Tasking modules that support data exchange and command input necessary for optimization.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and document the interface protocols and data exchange frequency between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking to manage interface complexity.
Assess and mitigate potential overlap with the Vehicle module by defining clear boundaries or coordination mechanisms between sequencing logic and physical operation constraints.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the functional requirement with a logically sound design parameter and a well-contained implementation module. Verification is feasible, and allocation clarity is established. Although there are some interface complexity and diffusion risks, these are manageable through recommended revisions without necessitating further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and stable design at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Clarify and document interface protocols and data exchange frequency between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking modules to manage interface complexity; define clear boundaries or coordination mechanisms with the Vehicle module to mitigate overlap risks.

assumptions: The current DP effectively optimizes the load phase cycle time as required.
The NGHE Solution module can be clearly delineated from other modules despite some interface complexity.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are adequate and implementable.
open_risks: Potential overlap with Vehicle module if physical operation constraints are tightly coupled with sequencing logic.
Interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking modules may increase due to frequent updates or adjustments.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface protocols and data exchange frequency.
Defined boundaries or coordination mechanisms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Verification test results demonstrating cycle time p95 compliance and safety adherence.
